item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like how this silly idea is thought through to its logical conclusion. The bridge is also a very interesting note to end on, which can easily be expanded on, but it could have been better integrated into the rest of the werebrick facts. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember reading an article in the JIR about the rate of venereal disease in bricks, and it reporting a lone brick with VD. This explains that result.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's important to note that if enough of these werebricks got together, they could form a werehouse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Were…bricks…

The concept is so silly I'm not sure whether to upvote, downvote, or just laugh until my posterior becomes significantly lighter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too weird for me, but I can see the appeal. I kept saying "…really??" to myself as I went on. I just can't take it seriously, maybe as a -J

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
All in all, it's all just bricks in the-
*gets pelted with rotten fruit*

Werebricks has accumulated more than 100 upvotes.

Let that sink in.

Congrats.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the d between instinct and compulsion, depending on the definition of the anomaly. The whole thing… It's based on a gag. Can't fault that, but I'd say it's pretty limp for what it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the fuck. Upvoted with vigor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1+, but… What kinda drugs were you on? SCP 420-J? This is hillarious!

WEREBRICKS. What. How. WHo. Japan? Internet? ANYONE? How did you come up with this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, didn't really find this humorous or weird
Actually I kinda thought it was cool to witness another creativity, though it was not enough to propel me to upvote
My only nitpick is that the picture of bridge seems a bit poorly made - I thought there was some grey entity at the end of bridge, floating on the river doing something…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My thoughts exactly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some stupid, stupid, stupid reason, the bridge really creeps me out. The inanity and silliness of were-bricks is going ignored, here. I upvoted for the bridge.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have found my people. Awoo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was…fun. And kind of oddly melancholic with the bridge. I think I like it

+1

Also, is the mortar in the picture non-anomalous?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See, I did totally miss that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Teamwork was mentioned.

This generally requires teamwork between multiple instances

And the bricks don't throw themselves, they do that in human form and then transform while falling:

single instance throwing itself from a vantage point and transmogrifying mid-fall

Also, they only transform when they see the full moon. And they can start building the bridge before the moon is out, as the article specifically states they start building the day before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Totally WTF, and I love it. Especially this:

MTF Beta-13 ("Over Troubled Waters").

Tee hee

Sirpudding: Yeah, I know. Me myself I, I just wrote re: another skip that I always pay attention to the believablitiy of stories… You know what - who cares? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Throwing themselves is more motion than bricks are ordinarily capable of. Regardless, if non-transforned instances are wielding transformed instances as weapons and building them into bridges, this ought to be mentioned as it is pretty significant. For one thing, it renders them completely harmless during the full moon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The way I understand it, they're not capable of motion. They have to either drop themselves from heights and transform mid-fall to bludgeon people or have others help (notice that the man on the right of the first picture is in fact another instance).

The bridge is then presumably built by human instances maneuvering transformed instances and letting them seal together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, I am maybe being dumb because I read this three times and there's no mention of how, or even if, the bricks are capable of motion. This seems to be too important a detail to omit, given that we are told they infect victims by bludgeoning and are somehow building a bridge. -1 on this basis, for now anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry, werebricks are just too silly for me.

inb4 nofunallowed.png

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd have thought to go with this instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hate you so much for taking my stupid in-joke and turning it into a thing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm very much reminded of Adventure Time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let them build the bridge. I want to know where it goes, damn it. Let them build the goddamn bridge!

+1, jerk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… what? This is just weird, man. I'm not sure in a good way, either.

It's a pretty straightforward brickification of a standard werecritter up until that last bit about the bridge, which itself just seems kinda… there. Maybe if there was some kind of explanation about the other side of the bridge, even just a brief summary of how it was changing instead of just THAT it was changing.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Challenge: write a supernatural romance story where one of the love interests is a were-brick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So this made me chuckle more than a main list article has any right to, as there is no getting around how silly the basic premise is. However, for me it is that silly premise that makes the creepy aspects of this article shine all the brighter.

People falling from great heights, turning into bricks and clobbering people. Bricks shattering on pavement and then turning into shattered people. For how brief this article is, you sure do pack in a lot of vibrant imagery for the reader to pick through.

+1. This was fun, TG.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neat little SCP. I really enjoyed it.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so incredibly silly. I'm not even sure if to upvote, downvote or enjoy the fuck out of that reference.

+-0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fun fact: Twistedgears was inspired to write this by an IRC quote from me that made reference to a running joke within another IRC group. A few days later, this happened.

I like this, I think it's a good concept, and the idea of how conversion works is just… a fantastic image. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some fitting music while listening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bring out your iron stakes, boys, we're hunting werebricks tonight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So any clue on what they're trying to build a bridge to?

item.title: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded:
Made this on a whim after a bot spit out a quote that contained the word "werebricks." The mental image was so great I had to do it. Thanks to djkaktus for the first image and some brainstorming, TyGently and Decibelle for looking at the draft, and PickYerPoison for being a supercoolguy and making the second image.

Image sources:

First image by Luigi Novi, cropped by djkaktus

This image taken by Steven Daniels. and this image by Andrew Shiva, edited by PickYerPoison

The quote was from TyGently, and pulled from a bot made by GreenWolf. I guess GreenWolf also made the original joke? Also DiePotato liked the joke.
